title: Co-immunoprecipitation using GFP-trap
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"3p019","text":"Here, we describe performing co-immunoprecipitation experiments in HEK293 cells using GFP-trap beads","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: See "Protocol: HEK293 cell culture for co-immunoprecipitation experiments" for preceding culture and transfection. For the present study, HEK cells were transfected with EGFP-tagged bait proteins. Prey proteins were either endogenous or transfected with HA- or SNAP-tags.

Prepare wash buffer: A B 1 Tris-HCl pH 7.5 10 mM 2 NaCl 150 mM 3 EDTA 0.5 mM 4 Triton-X (optional) 0.4% Triton-X should be included for prey proteins where appreciable non-specific binding is observed in the EGFP vector condition.
Prepare lysis buffer. Lysis buffer composition differed for experiments needing lambda phosphatase treatment
Lysis buffer for non-lambda phosphatase experiment: A B 1 Tris-HCl pH 7.5 10 mM 2 NaCl 150 mM 3 EDTA 0.5 mM 4 NP-40 0.5% 5 PMSF 1 mM 6 TAME 0.01 mg/mL 7 Leupeptin 0.01 mg/mL 8 Pepstatin A 0.001 mg/mL
Lysis buffer compatible with lambda phosphatase experiment: A B 1 1x NEBuffer for Protein MettaloPhosphatases (New England BioLabs) 1x 2 NP-40 0.5% 3 Leupeptin 0.01 mg/mL 4 ddH2O To desired volume
24 hours after transfection, wash HEK cells twice in ice-cold PBS and lyse in appropriate lysis buffer. Use 600 µL lysis buffer per experimental condition (pooled across the three 10cm dishes).
Clarify lysates at 10 x g at 4 degrees C for 10 minutes

Wash 25 µL GFP-trap beads per experimental condition in 500 µL wash buffer, in low protein binding Eppendorf tubes under rotating agitation. For large protein complexes, GFP-Trap Magnetic Particles M-270 should be used instead of GFP-Trap Magnetic Agarose beads
Equilbrate beads in lysis buffer for 5 min at 4 degrees C under rotating agitation
Incubate beads with clarified lysate (or lambda phosphatase-treated lysate) for 1 hour at 4 degrees C under rotating agitation

For lambda phosphatase experiments: add 60 µL of 10 mM MnCl 2 and 24 µL lambda phosphatase (2,400 units; New England BioLabs) for a final reaction volume of 600 µL per experimental condition. For conditions that are lambda phosphatase-negative, add 24 µL ddH 2 O instead
Wash beads three times for 5 min in wash buffer at 4 degrees C under rotating agitation
Incubate at 30 degrees C for 30 minutes
Resuspend beads in 60 µL denaturing buffer, and boil for 10 minutes to release bound protein
creator: Dan Dou
